figures.caption: Fig. 2 Dependence of (a) p in (25) and (b) s in (27) on the index q with solid symbols representing the values considered in the present study. The dashed horizontal lines correspond to the limiting values of p and s obtained when q → ∞.

Fig. 7 [q = 3, Problem 3, B = 562.34 and T = 5×10−5] Snapshots of the magnitude of the vorticity |ω(tix)| (red color scale) in the solution of the Navier-Stokes system (1) along with vortex lines (red) and the quantity |u(ti,x)|3 (blue color scale) shown at the times t0, ..., t2. These time instances are marked with solid black symbols in Figure 4(c).

Fig. 8 [q = 3, Problem 4, B = 707.10 and T = 5×10−5] Snapshots of the magnitude of the vorticity |ω(ti,x)| (red color scale) in the solution of the Navier-Stokes system (1) along with vortex lines (red) and the quantity |u(ti,x)|3 (blue color scale) shown at the times t0, ..., t2. These time instances are marked with solid green symbols in Figure 4(e).

Fig. 12 [q = 9, Problem 1, B = 800 and T = 2× 10−4] Snapshots of the magnitude of the vorticity |ω(ti,x)| (red color scale) in the solution of the Navier-Stokes system (1) along with vortex lines (red) and the quantity |u(ti,x)|9 (blue color scale) shown at the times t0, ..., t3. These time instances are marked with solid black symbols in Figure 9(c).

Fig. 11 [q = 9] Navier-Stokes flows corresponding to the optimal initial conditions found by solving Problem 1 and 2 for different B and T shown using the coordinates (a) { ||u(t)||L9 , d dt ||u(t)||L9 } and (b) {E(t), dE(t)/dt}. The black solid lines represent the upper bounds (a) d dt ||u(t)||L9 ∼ ||u(t)||4 L9 from (19) and (b) dE/dt ∼ E3 from (11). The dashed lines show the relations (a) d dt ||u(t)||L9 ∼ ||u(t)||5/2 L9 from (22) and (b) dE/dt ∼ E2. Trajectories marked in blue and red correspond to solutions of Problems 1 and 2, respectively. The intensity of the color is related to the length of the time window with darker colors corresponding to solutions obtained on longer time windows T .

Fig. 4 [q = 3] Time evolution of (a,c,e) the norm ||u(t)||L3 and (b,d,f) the normalized enstrophy E(u(t))/E(0) in the Navier-Stokes flows with optimal initial conditions obtained by solving Problem 3 (blue) and Problem 4 (red). The values of the constraint parameter are (a), (b) B = 376.06, (c), (d) B = 562.34 and (e), (f) B = 707.10. Solutions are computed over the time window [0, T ] where T takes three representative values, including the one which gives approximately the largest value of ΨT (ũ0;B,T ) for a given value of B. The solid symbols in (c) and (e) mark the time instances at which the flows are visualized in Figures 7–8.

Fig. 5 [q = 3] (a) Dependence of the local maxima of the objective functional (26) on the length T of the optimization window in Problems 3 (blue) and 4 (red) for different values of the constraint B = 376.06, 562.34, 707.10 with the arrow indicating the trend with the increase of B. (b) Dependence of maxT ΨT ( ũ0;B,T ) on B3 = ||ũ0;B,T ||3 L3 with solid lines representing the power-law fits (66a)–(66b).

Fig. 10 [q = 9] (a) Dependence of the local maxima of the objective functional Φ9 T (ũ0;B,T ) on the length T of the optimization window in Problems 1 (blue) and 2 (red) for different values of the constraint B = 500, 800, 1200 with the arrow indicating the trend with the increase of B. (b) Dependence of maxT Φ9 T ( ũ0;B,T ) on B3 = ||ũ0;B,T ||3 L9 with solid lines representing the power-law fits (67a)–(67b).

Fig. 14 (a) Dependence of the exponent γ in expressions (68) on q for flows with optimal initial conditions found by solving (blue) Problems 1 and 3 and (red) Problems 2 and 4. (b) Dependence of ∆E, cf. (69), on the minimum enstrophy Emin in flows with the optimal initial conditions found by solving (blue) Problems 1 and 3, and (red) Problems 2 and 4. In (b), each symbol corresponds to a different value of q: (squares) q = 3, (circles) q = 4, (triangles) q = 5 and (diamonds) q = 9. The solid blue line represents the relation ∆E ∼ CE3/2 min for some C > 0.

Fig. 9 [q = 9] Time evolution of (a,c,e) the norm ||u(t)||L9 and (b,d,f) the normalized enstrophy E(u(t))/E(0) in the Navier-Stokes flows with optimal initial conditions obtained by solving Problem 1 (blue) and Problem 2 (red). The values of the constraint parameter are (a), (b) B = 500, (c) , (d) B = 800 and (e), (f) B = 1200. Solutions are computed over the time window [0, T ] where T takes three representative values, including the one which gives approximately the largest value of Φ9 T (ũ0;B,T ) for a given value of B. The solid symbols in (c) mark the time instances at which the flows are visualized in Figures 12–13 .

Fig. 6 [q = 3] Navier-Stokes flows corresponding to the optimal initial conditions found by solving Problem 3 and 4 for different B and T shown using the coordinates (a) { ||u(t)||L3 , d dt ||u(t)||L3 } and (b) {E(t), dE(t)/dt}. In (b) the black solid line shows the upper bound dE/dt ∼ E3 on the rate of change of enstrophy, cf. (11). The dashed lines show the relations (a) d dt ||u(t)||L3 ∼ ||u(t)||5 L3 from (22) and (b) dE/dt ∼ E2. Trajectories marked in blue and red correspond to solutions of Problems 3 and 4, respectively. The intensity of the color is related to the length of the time window with darker colors corresponding to solutions obtained on longer time windows T .

Fig. 13 [q = 9, Problem 2, B = 800 and T = 2× 10−4] Snapshots of the magnitude of the vorticity |ω(ti,x)| (red color scale) in the solution of the Navier-Stokes system (1) along with vortex lines (red) and the quantity |u(ti,x)|9 (blue color scale) shown at the times t0, ..., t3. These time instances are marked with solid green symbols in Figure 9(c).
